title: carduus defloratus subsp. carlinifolius
intro: Carduus defloratus subsp. carlinifolius, also known as the Carline Thistle, is a subspecies of the Carduus defloratus plant, characterized by its distinctive leaves and a range of medicinal properties. The Carline Thistle is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is also valued for its ability to support the immune system and promote overall health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions. In traditional herbal medicine, the Carline Thistle is often used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for a range of health conditions, and is also used to make herbal teas for their health benefits.
